metadata.title: OpenAI's for-profit conversion & the $1.4T-vs-$25B gap — the engine of the circular core
metadata.status: facts from CNBC/Fortune/OpenAI/Microsoft; the '~56x commitments-to-revenue gap is the bubble's load-bearing number' framing is labeled interpretation
metadata.frame: OpenAI is the node every other node in the circular core booked as backlog/RPO/expected revenue. This block covers the Oct-2025 for-profit conversion that unlocked the mega-capital, the resulting governance structure, and the central number: ~$1.4T of compute commitments against a ~$25B revenue run-rate (~56x). The conversion is what made the Microsoft/MGX/SoftBank/Gulf capital legally raisable; the gap is what the Z3 proofs (T3 needs >=$1.03T external; T4 insolvent at zero inflow) formalize. Overlay; excluded from the proofs (but the proofs operate on these figures). Connects to fin-microsoft-openai, fin-coreweave-oracle, fin-gulf-sovereign-ai-capital, fin-ai-depreciation-debttrap.

the_conversion.what: 28 Oct 2025: OpenAI completed its for-profit recapitalization. The for-profit arm is now OpenAI Group PBC (a public benefit corporation); the OpenAI Foundation (nonprofit) holds ~26% as a CONTROLLING stake; current/former employees + investors hold ~47%; Microsoft holds ~27% (~$135B). status: fact.
the_conversion.capped_profit_gone: The old 'capped-profit' cap was abandoned for a conventional equity structure (nonprofit-controls-PBC) - the change that made unlimited upside, and therefore the mega-rounds, possible. status: fact.
the_conversion.regulatory: It cleared the Delaware Attorney General (a 'statement of no objection') and California review - the charitable-asset-conversion scrutiny resolved in OpenAI's favor. status: fact + contested (critics argued a charitable mission was converted to private gain).

why_the_conversion_matters: The nonprofit structure capped how much capital OpenAI could raise; converting to a PBC with normal equity is what UNLOCKED the Microsoft 27% recap, the MGX/SoftBank/Gulf mega-rounds (fin-gulf-sovereign-ai-capital), and an eventual IPO path (reported ~$850B valuation talk). The conversion is the legal precondition for the 'continuous external-capital injection' the proofs require - no conversion, no $1.4T of commitments.

the_central_gap.commitments: OpenAI's compute commitments total on the order of $1.4 TRILLION: Stargate ($500B headline / 10GW with Oracle + SoftBank, Jan 2025), an Oracle agreement >$300B over five years (Jul 2025), plus Azure ($250B), Broadcom/AMD/Nvidia/CoreWeave. By Sep 2025 Stargate alone claimed ~7GW planned and >$400B committed over three years. status: fact.
the_central_gap.revenue: OpenAI's annualized revenue run-rate was reported above ~$25B by end-Feb 2026. status: fact.
the_central_gap.the_ratio: ~$1.4T of commitments vs ~$25B of revenue is a ~56x gap - the bubble's load-bearing number. Every counterparty (Oracle's $523B RPO, CoreWeave's 67%-one-customer book, Nvidia's vendor financing) has booked a slice of that $1.4T as its own backlog/expected revenue. status: fact (figures) + interpretation (the 'load-bearing' read).

moral_authority_zhengming.summary: #55 - the moral-authority / 正名 (zhengming, 'rectification of names') angle: OpenAI's legitimacy is borrowed from a founding NAME and mission the new structure contradicts. The founding facts + the conversion + the Musk suit are fact; the 正名 framing and the 'moral-capital spend' read are labeled interpretation.
moral_authority_zhengming.the_founding_name: OpenAI was founded (2015) as a NONPROFIT 'to ensure that artificial general intelligence benefits all of humanity,' explicitly NOT driven by 'the need to generate financial return' - and 'Open' in the name signaled openness/transparency. Its moral authority (talent, partnerships, regulatory forbearance, a 'safety-first' halo) draws on that name. status: fact.
moral_authority_zhengming.the_name_reality_gap: The Oct-2025 conversion makes the reality diverge from the name: a PBC with conventional equity, the nonprofit reduced to a ~26% 'controlling' stake, Microsoft ~27%, employees/investors ~47%, capped-profit abandoned, an IPO path (~$850B talk), and most models neither open-source nor open-weight. The name ('Open', 'nonprofit', 'benefit of humanity') no longer rectifies the thing. status: fact (the structure) + labeled (the gap).
moral_authority_zhengming.zhengming_frame: Confucian 正名: when names no longer match reality, 'language loses meaning' and trust/legitimacy erode - and a ruler's authority depends on names being rectified. Applied here: OpenAI raises capital and recruits on the moral capital of the original name while the governance contradicts it. This is the MORAL analogue of the financial gap - just as $1.4T commitments >> $25B revenue (promises > substance), the mission claims >> the governance substance. The 'nonprofit controls the PBC' assertion is itself a self-marked claim about control that a safety-vs-shipping forcing event would test.
moral_authority_zhengming.documented_dispute: The mission-betrayal claim is litigated, not just rhetorical: Elon Musk sued Sam Altman/OpenAI alleging the for-profit turn betrayed the founding charitable mission; the Delaware AG issued a 'statement of no objection' and California reviewed - critics (incl. former staff and nonprofit-law scholars) argued a charitable mission was converted to private gain. status: fact (the suit + the reviews) + contested (whether it is a betrayal).
moral_authority_zhengming.why_it_matters_financially: Moral authority is not decorative - it is a FUNDING input: the safety/benefit halo underwrites talent retention, the Microsoft/Gulf partnerships' public legitimacy, regulatory forbearance, and the 'public benefit' framing that smooths the charitable-asset conversion. The conversion SPENDS that moral capital to unlock the financial capital the $1.4T commitments require. It is the self_marked_value defect at the level of LEGITIMACY: a chosen label (nonprofit/benefit/AGI-safety) held above its realizable meaning until a forcing event makes mission and profit openly conflict.
moral_authority_zhengming.grade: founding mission + conversion structure + Musk suit + AG/CA reviews FACT; the 正名 framing and the moral-capital-spend read are labeled interpretation

how_it_ties_to_the_proofs: The formal core operates exactly here: Z3 T3 proves OpenAI needs >=$1.03T of EXTERNAL capital to honor its commitments (UNSAT without it); T4 proves the core is insolvent at zero external inflow; TLA+ shows a capital-stop cascading OpenAI->CoreWeave->Oracle. The for-profit conversion is what keeps the external tap legally open; the Gulf (fin-gulf-sovereign-ai-capital) is who is increasingly pouring through it; and the depreciation trap (fin-ai-depreciation-debttrap) means the compute the $1.4T buys ages out in ~2-3 years. The governance change, the capital source, the commitments, and the asset life are one story: a mission-born nonprofit restructured to raise the unprecedented external capital its commitments require - solvent only while it keeps arriving.
